data.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G3 (child coverage) / parent 5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa + this child.

VERDICT: No parent acceptance/success criterion is left UNCOVERED on account of this pairing. But this child contributes ZERO net coverage AND is INCONSISTENT with the parent's own settled design — recording one finding so the coverage map is honest.

FINDING — child contradicts the parent it was filed to cover; its lane is already covered by siblings (so it is a dropped/superseded over-call, not a coverage contributor)
- criteria: [G3, G4]
- location: child ce75 §Scope / §Why ("a documented rollback / back-out path", "system-level kill switch", "Deploy ordering for the signature scheme") vs parent 5fd2 Success Criterion #4 + §"Design clarification — rollback is config-off".
- evidence:
  1. PARENT EXPLICITLY SAYS THE OPPOSITE. Parent SC#4: "'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, NOT a deploy-time kill-switch, and NO SEPARATE ROLLBACK MECHANISM IS NEEDED." Parent §Design summary likewise: the gate's posture makes config-off the rollback. This child's premise ("a gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure"; demands "a documented rollback / back-out path" + "system-level kill switch") asserts a gap the parent design already closed by construction.
  2. CHILD CLOSED WON'T-DO/SUPERSEDED. ce75's own resolution comment (Joe's adjudication): "config-gating IS the kill-switch/rollback... No separate rollback mechanism is needed... advisory (not block) over-call the Pass-2 verifier's 'no-existing-mitigation' check should have softened." FORCE_CLOSE with no signature. So the child delivers nothing toward any parent criterion.
  3. THE PARENT CRITERIA IN THIS CHILD'S LANE ARE COVERED BY SIBLINGS:
     - Gate ON/OFF config preference + "off => claim unchanged (fail-open)" (ce75 AC#1) is delivered by sibling 092b-3bb7-4890-4cb9 — added AC "ENFORCE the config preference (defaults OFF): off → claim/transition unchanged; on → the fast signature check gates them" (verify-completion PASS; config.py require_plan_review_for_claim default False, tested). The config TOOL/UX is the separate epic 55de (out of scope per parent).
     - Deploy ordering / old+new coexistence for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events (ce75 AC#2/AC#3 lane) is delivered by sibling db7b-c8fd-190f-4506 — added AC "ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence: ... preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones ... fsck WARNs on newer types ... readers-before-writers" (verify-completion PASS).
- scenarios: A reviewer trusting the registry could read ce75's title ("T12 gap the gate found on itself") and conclude the parent has an unmet rollback/kill-switch criterion. It does not: the parent settled this as config-off (SC#4), and the residual mechanics (preference enforcement on the claim path; event rollout-ordering) are covered by 092b + db7b.
- impact: No coverage gap and no orphaned parent criterion; the risk is a CONSISTENCY/registry-hygiene one — a closed-won't-do child whose framing (parent has an unaddressed rollback gap) directly conflicts with parent SC#4. It should not be read as evidence that the parent's rollback/kill-switch criterion is unowned.
- checklist_item: "[ ] Confirm no parent rollback/kill-switch criterion is left uncovered by ce75's closure: SC#4 ('config-off IS the rollback, no separate mechanism needed') + sibling 092b (enforce gate-off preference on claim path) + sibling db7b (REVIEW_RESULT/signature rollout + readers-before-writers coexistence) together cover this lane; ce75 added nothing and was correctly superseded."
- suggested_fix: Leave ce75 closed-superseded (correct disposition). For registry honesty, the parent already records this in SC#4 + the "rollback is config-off" Design clarification; no parent-criterion remediation is required. If anything, link ce75 as superseded_by/relates_to 092b + db7b so the supersession trail is explicit rather than only in the close comment.

G3 NET: parent acceptance/success criteria in this child's concern-area (gate ON/OFF preference; rollout-ordering/coexistence of the sidecar+signature events) are fully covered — by parent SC#4's design stance plus siblings 092b and db7b — so there is NO uncovered parent criterion to flag against the roster. This child is a superseded over-call, consistent only after recognizing config-off as the parent's intended rollback.
